subject: Tie XACML REST Decision
commit_message:
Tie XACML REST Decision

Ties the decision API to the applications. Adds a static
application manager class to manager the applications.

Added applicationPath as a parameter to be supported in
the Json parameter group.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1440
Change-Id: I43cb44b3e308aadcf258fd987f3b53944c2edb72
Signed-off-by: Pamela Dragosh <pdragosh@research.att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Could someone help me with this? I am trying to get ErrorResponse object. But invocation builder throws an exception. Also tried running this as an application but Jackson threw an exception when serializing the object. That I think is a different issue.

Patch Set 1:

When I run as a java application, my POST returns this exception:
/policy/pdpx/v1/decision com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.UnrecognizedPropertyException: Unrecognized field "ONAPName" (class org.onap.policy.models.decisions.concepts.DecisionRequest), not marked as ignorable (6 known properties: "onapInstance", "action", "onapName", "requestId", "resource", "onapComponent"])
 at [Source: (org.glassfish.jersey.message.internal.ReaderInterceptorExecutor$UnCloseableInputStream); line: 2, column: 16] (through reference chain: org.onap.policy.models.decisions.concepts.DecisionRequest["ONAPName"])

Patch Set 1:

Why does it use jackson? I've used gson for annotations in the class? Wondering if this is another issue with xacml jackson dependency.

Patch Set 1:

> Why does it use jackson? I've used gson for annotations in the
 > class? Wondering if this is another issue with xacml jackson
 > dependency.

I know policy/engine still uses jackson.  However, if you think this should work with gson, and it's based on the JettyServer classes in policy-endpoints, then try setting this property:
  
        props.setProperty("your-service-name" + PolicyEndPointProperties.PROPERTY_HTTP_SERIALIZATION_PROVIDER,
                        GsonMessageBodyHandler.class.getName());

Patch Set 1:

fyi: JettyServer still defaults to jackson, until we can get everything converted.  Maybe in El Alto, we should make gson the default and add this property where jackson is still needed.

Patch Set 1:

I think we gotta make this change now - because it just doesn't work.

Patch Set 1:

or do I add jackson annotation to DecisionRequest class?

Patch Set 1:

or if I just add a class like this will that fix it?
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9516224/using-gson-instead-of-jackson-in-jersey

Patch Set 1:

> or if I just add a class like this will that fix it?
 > https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9516224/using-gson-instead-of-jackson-in-jersey

yeah, kind of went down that path but it didn't work very well.  I need to know a little more about the situation.  Let me pull this repo...

Patch Set 1:

Try adding this after line 116 of XacmlPdpRestServer.java:

        props.setProperty(HTTP_SERVER_SERVICES + SEPARATOR + restServerParameters.getName() + ".serialization.provider",
                GsonMessageBodyHandler.class.getName());

If that doesn't fix it, then you may indeed have found the xacml jar has a hard dependency on jackson.

Patch Set 1:

Yes!!! That did it - it worked via Postman. Now I have to figure out the JUnit tests. Or I skip testing error response in there for the moment.

Looks good to me.. I am wondering, since I used your locateExistingPolicyTypes() method in the XacmlPdpMessage class I created to structure messages to the PAP, if we can just add a piece of code here that would add the supportedPolicyTypes to the policyTypeIdents list used in registration and PdpStatus messages to the PAP.  Then I can remove that method and just get the policyTypeIdents from here rather than have redundant code in the XacmlPdpMessage class.  If you agree, I can make the change and submit it with my next patch to my review for registration today.
